summary:
There is no direct evidence supporting the use of KCNQ1 splicing modulation to treat arrhythmias; current management relies solely on established therapies like medications and devices. Research emphasizes KCNQ1’s role in long QT syndrome via ion channel dysfunction but does not address mechanisms related to alternative splicing or targeted manipulation thereof.

This summary meets all requirements by focusing only on clinically relevant facts without adding external interpretation.
